scope: PHN-2529 - GET /sales-portal/customer/by-coordinates dependency and impact analysis across controller, service, DTO/projection, and repository native query layers.

upstream.name: Auth chain (gateway/security filter/JWT validation)
upstream.usage: Bearer token validation (signature/issuer/audience/scope/role) is evaluated before business logic and can short-circuit request flow with 401/403.

upstream.name: Request parameter binding and validation
upstream.usage: Coordinates and pagination contract depends on query params latitude/longitude/radius/page/size/sort and framework validation constraints.

upstream.name: CustomerDetailsRepository native SQL + count query
upstream.usage: Business data, classification derivation, and pagination metadata depend on SQL joins/filters/order and count query parity.

upstream.name: phoenix-core-lib shared request/response/projection models
upstream.usage: DTO/projection schema contracts are shared across modules and must stay compatible with phoenix-core runtime mapping.

downstream.name: Sales Portal UI/API clients
downstream.usage: Consumers depend on canonical endpoint path, auth behavior, and stable response schema for list rendering and pagination UX.

downstream.name: EnergoTS automated tests (PHN-2529 suite)
downstream.usage: Tests assert status codes, classification values, and pagination consistency; failures surface contract/auth drift quickly.

downstream.name: Downstream analytics/reporting consumers
downstream.usage: Any consumer using classification labels (acquisition/recontract) or paging metadata is affected by response/logic changes.

integration_points: API gateway/security filter chain to controller dispatch
Controller -> Service -> Repository native query execution path
Shared model compatibility between phoenix-core-lib and phoenix-core
Endpoint path consistency across backend routing, docs, and clients
Stable sorting and count-query parity for pagination correctness

what_could_break.reason: Valid requests can be rejected with 401/403 if token scope/issuer/audience/role expectations drift.

what_could_break.location: Path mapping and consumers for /customer vs /customers variants
what_could_break.reason: If only one path is wired/documented, clients or tests can fail with 404/501 or call wrong endpoint.
Join/CASE/filter changes can mislabel acquisition/recontract and break business semantics.
Count query mismatch, unstable ordering, or projection mapping drift can cause overlap/gaps or incorrect totalElements/totalPages.
Version skew between DTO/projection definitions and runtime mapper/controller can break deserialization/field expectations.
Invalid/missing coordinate or pagination params may return unexpected status codes if validation order/constraints are changed.
